Value.data: Local Discovery Server URL
The Local Discovery server to connect to to configure Server URL.
Server Test
Server URL

Important! The UA Server to connect to for testing.
TIP: copy/paste your UA Server URL here and replace 'localhost' with actual host name!
TIP: specify 'opc.tcp://localhost:4842' to connect via another UACTT to trace calls.
Default Subscription Publish Interval

Default publishing interval for Subscriptions (in msec)
Recommended value: 250
Important: This value has a direct impact on how much time the Subscription tests will take.
DiagnosticInfo Response Testing

Enable/Disable scripts that test validity of DiagnosticInfo in Response Headers.
Recommended value: Checked
Time Tolerance

The amount of time to allow for transactions to occur.
TIP: Default value is 100.
Subscription Timeout

TOTAL subscription timeout (in seconds). This value is used to automatically set the MaxKeepAliveCount and LifetimeCounter.
Recommended value: 15
Important: Set this value high (e.g. 180) if you are debugging your Server, otherwise set it low.
Time Synchronization Checking

Checked = Read the clock time on the Server and Client to determine the delay. Significant delays can cause failures.
Unchecked = do not check the clock.
TIP: Default value is checked (true).
Max String Length

The maximum length of a string value.
TIP: 0 means no limit or the limit is greater than 65534
ATTENTION: Reliability cannot be guaranteed for values higher than 65534.
Max Supported Subscriptions

The MAXIMUM number of Subscriptions supported.
Important: This value will be tested.
UA Sample Server setting available in: Opc.Ua.SampleServer.Config.xml
Max Supported MonitoredItems

The Maximum number of MonitoredItems per Subscription that the server supports.
TIP: 0 means no limit
The value will be limited to a suitable value for the CTT!
Retransmission QueueSize per Session

How many publish requests per session, i.e. size of the retransmission queue.
Default value: 10
Fastest Publish Interval Supported

The fastest value (msecs) the UA Server supports for the PublishingInterval.
Please specify the fastest Publishing interval your UA Server supports.
Some scripts will use this value to determine if the server revises the samplingInterval to this value.
Fastest Sampling Interval Supported

The fastest value (msecs) the UA Server supports for a monitored item SamplingInterval.
Some scripts will use this value to determine if the server revises the samplingInterval to match this value.
Max Publish Requests per Session

The maximum number of queued Publish calls allowed per session.
TIP: a value of 0 means no limit.
Max SecureChannels
The MAXIMUM number of secure channels that your server supports.
SecurityNone Enabled

SecurityNone should be disabled by default.
Checked: SecurityNone is enabled because the user has specifically enabled it.
Unchecked: SecurityNone is not specifically enabled and therefore should be unavailable.
Max Supported Sessions

The MAX # of Sessions the Server supports.
Note: 0 = means no limit.
GoodCompletesAsynchronously delay
The time in miliseconds which is needed by the server to ensure that the written value has been set in the underlying device.
Secure Channel

The level of Security to use when transmitting UA messages, such as None, Encrypt, Sign & Encrypt etc.
TIP: You should experiment with this setting if you are using a Custom UA Stack.
Recommended value: None

The time (msecs) to allow for a Network Timeout to occur.
Recommended value: 2000

The RequestedLifetime value when determining the lifetime of a channel.
Recommended value: 30000
For advanced users only!
Min Lifetime of SecureChannel
The time in miliseconds before the server reuses an unused SecureChannel.

The default RequestedSecurityPolicyUri that the UA CTT will specify when connecting to a UA Server.
TIP: You should experiment with this setting if using a custom UA Stack.
Recommended value: None

This setting will be used by the 'ActivateSession' calls in test-scripts.
TIP: make sure the server supports the selected user authentication policy.

Specify an user that has permission to activate a session and to read the SessionSecurityDiagnostics Array.
TIP: only needed if the server supports username/password user authentication.

Password associated with LoginNameGranted1.
TIP: only needed if the server supports username/password user authentication.

Specify an alternate user that has permission to activate a session.
TIP: only needed if the server supports username/password user authentication.

Password associated with LoginNameGranted2.
TIP: only needed if the server supports username/password user authentication.

Specify an user that does not have permission to activate a session.
TIP: only needed if the server supports username/password user authentication.
NOTE: Specify "n/a" if your server does not provide the ability to deny user-access to the server.

Password associated with LoginNameAccessDenied.
TIP: only needed if the server supports username/password user authentication.
NOTE: Specify "n/a" if your server does not provide the ability to deny user-access to the server.

The default TimeoutHint (in msec) that will be set in the buildRequestHeader() script.
Recommended value: 20000

The default RequestedSessionTimeout value as used by the test-scripts.
Recommended value: 60000

The MaxResponseMessageSize that the UA CTT will request (UA CTT is the Client).
Recommended value: 0
For advanced users only!
All Profiles

The NodeId of a Node whose Value attribute is of type: BOOL
Access: Read/Write

The NodeId of a Node whose Value attribute is of type: BYTE
Access: Read/Write

The NodeId of a Node whose Value attribute is of type: BYTESTRING

The NodeId of a Node whose Value attribute is of type: DATETIME
Access: Read/Write

The NodeId of a Node whose Value attribute is of type: DOUBLE
Access: Read/Write

The NodeId of a Node whose Value attribute is of type: DURATION
Access: Read/Write
Leave empty if not supported.

Access: Read/Write

The NodeId of a Node whose Value attribute is of type: GUID
Access: Read/Write

The NodeId of a Node type:INTEGER
Access: Read/Write
Leave empty if your system does not support this type

Access: Read/Write

The NodeId of a Node of a subtype of the type: ENUMERATION

The NodeId of a Node of type: IMAGE

The NodeId of a Node of type: DECIMAL
Access: Read/Write

The NodeId of a Node where the Value attribute is of Type: BOOL[]
Access: Read/Write
Preferably a Node that supports writes with an IndexRange, if supported.

The NodeId of a Node where the Value attribute is of Type: BYTE[]
Access: Read/Write
Preferably a Node that supports writes with an IndexRange, if supported.

Access: Read/Write
Preferably a Node that supports writes with an IndexRange, if supported.

The NodeId of a Node where the Value attribute is of Type: DATETIME[]
Access: Read/Write
Preferably a Node that supports writes with an IndexRange, if supported.
Leave empty if your system does not support the full-resolution of a DateTime, i.e. 100-nanosecond intervals from 1601.

Access: Read/Write

HA Profile
Specify a structure node supporting history.
Node of type Bool[]
Node of type Byte[]
Node of type ByteString[]
Node of Type DateTime[]
Node of type Double[]
Node of type Float[]
Node of type Int16[]
Node of type Int32[]
Node of type Int64[]
Node of type SByte[]
Node of type String[]
Node of type UInt16[]
Node of type UInt32[]
Node of type UInt64[]
Node of type XmlElement[]
NodeId of type Boolean, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type Byte, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type ByteString, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type DateTime, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type Double, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type Float, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type Int16, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type Int32, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type Int64, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type SByte, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type String, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type UInt16, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type UInt32, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type UInt64, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId of type XmlElement, that has 2-dimensions e.g. [5,5]
NodeId whose AccessLevel attribute is set to Read only. The UserAccessLevel is not considered.
NodeId whose AccessLevel attribute is set to Write only. The UserAccessLevel is not considered.
NodeId whose AccessLevel attribute is set to None. The UserAccessLevel attribute is not considered.
NodeId whose UserAccessLevel attribute is set to Read only. The AccessLevel attribute can be ReadWrite or ReadOnly.
NodeId whose UserAccessLevel attribute is set to Write only. The AccessLevel attribute can be ReadWrite or WriteOnly.
NodeId whose UserAccessLevel attribute is set to None. The AccessLevel attribute can be anything else.
NodeId that does not support TimestampsToReturn.Server.

Checked: The HA scripts will log verbose information - helpful when debugging.
Clear: Do not log verbose information during HA testing.
The default processing interval to be used in Aggregate requests
This time should indicates data of bad quality for Boolean One.
This time should indicates data of bad quality for Boolean Two.
This time should indicates data of bad quality for Integer One.
This time should indicates data of bad quality for Integer One.
This time should indicates data of bad quality for Float One.
This time should indicates data of bad quality for Float Two.
This time should indicates data of bad quality for Double One.
This time should indicates data of bad quality for Double Two.
This time should indicates data of bad quality for NonNumeric One.
This time should indicates data of bad quality for NonNumeric Two.

This time is used as StartTime parameter of HistoricalReadRawRequest to cache all historical data.
Format: 1601-01-01T00:00:00.000Z
Has 3 Forward References 1
A NodeId that has at least three Forward references.
Has 3 Forward References 2
A NodeId that has at least three Forward references.
Has 3 Forward References 3
A NodeId that has at least three Forward references.
Has 3 Forward References 4
A NodeId that has at least three Forward references.
Has 3 Forward References 5
A NodeId that has at least three Forward references.
Has Inverse And Forward References
A NodeId that has more than one Forward reference, and at least one Inverse reference.
Has References of a ReferenceType and SubType

This needs to be an Object or Variable with multiple HierarchicalReferences. The Node needs to have References of a ReferenceType but also of a Subtype of the ReferenceType. (E.g.: HasComponent + HasAlarmSuppressionGroup)
Has 3 Inverse References 1
A NodeId that has at least three Inverse references.
Starting Node 1
A node to start browsing from. A node that has both a Forward and Inverse references, where there is a chain of references of at least 10 deep.
Unknown Path 1
A BrowseName that specified a non-existent namespace index.
Max Depth

The maximum depth that scripts should search when navigating the UA Server's address space.
TIP: 0 = no limit.
Recommended value: 10.
A NodeId whose NodeClass is of type "Variable".
A NodeId whose NodeClass is of type "Object".
A NodeId whose NodeClass is of type Method.
A NodeId whose NodeClass is of type "ObjectType".
A NodeId whose NodeClass is of type "VariableType".
A NodeId whose NodeClass is of type "ReferenceType".
A NodeId whose NodeClass is of type"DataType".
A NodeId whose NodeClass is of type "View".

A NodeId whose AccessLevel attribute contains 'CurrentRead'.
Note: The 'UserAccessLevel' should match the 'AccessLevel' so that it is not more restrictive.

A NodeId whose AccessLevel attribute contains 'CurrentWrite'.
Note: The 'UserAccessLevel' should match the 'AccessLevel' so that it is not more restrictive.
A NodeId whose AccessLevel attribute contains 'CurrentRead' but the 'UserAccessLevel' does NOT contain 'CurrentRead'.
A NodeId whose AccessLevel attribute contains 'CurrentWrite' but the 'UserAccessLevel' does NOT contain 'CurrentWrite'.
A NodeId whose UserAccessLevel contains CurrentRead, but explicitly does not include CurrentWrite.
A NodeId whose UserAccessLevel contains CurrentWrite, but explicitly does not include CurrentRead.
The NodeId of a node which allows for Nodes to be added and/or deleted.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Server allows the Client to specfiy this attribute type when adding a node.
Supports the "Organizes" reference
Supports the "HasEventSource" reference
Supports the "HasModellingRule" reference
Supports the "HasEncoding" reference
Supports the "HasDescription" reference
Supports the "HasTypeDefinition" reference
Supports the "GeneratesEvent" reference
Supports the "AlwaysGeneratesEvent" reference
Supports the "HasSubtype" reference
Supports the "HasProperty" reference
Supports the "HasComponent" reference
Supports the "HasNotifier" reference
Supports the "FromState" reference
Supports the "HasCause" reference
Supports the "HasEffect" reference
Supports the "HasSubStateMachine" reference
Supports the "HasHistoricalConfiguration" reference

Checked=Client will request the NodeId for newly created Nodes
Clear=Client will NOT specify a NodeId; the Server will generate the NodeId instead.
The namespace to use when adding nodes.

When selected, the Client-specified NodeId will be created using a STRING identifier
e.g. "ns=911;s=MyStringNodeId"

When selected, the Client-specified NodeId will be created using a NUMERIC identifier
e.g. "ns=911;i=100"

When selected, the Client-specified NodeId will be created using a GUID identifier
e.g. "ns=911;g={00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000}"
Supports adding nodes of class: DataType
Supports adding nodes of class: Method
Supports adding nodes of class: Object
Supports adding nodes of class: ObjectType
Supports adding nodes of class: ReferenceType
Supports adding nodes of class: Variable
Supports adding nodes of class: VariableType
Supports adding nodes of class: View
NodeId of a Method whose signature matches: void method();

NodeId of a Method whose signature has both in and out parameters, e.g. void method( in, out ).
The CTT will automatically utilize the parameters while respecting the data-types.

NodeId of a Method whose signature has in parameters only, e.g. void method( in ).
The CTT will automatically utilize the parameters while respecting the data-types.
NodeId of a Method whose signature has out parameters only, e.g. void method( out ).
Node that has multiple methods attached to it.
Node that will invoke an event (of any kind) whenever it is written to.
Node that will invoke an event (of any kind) whenever it is written to.
True = print verbose output for UA Service calls via library objects. False = print minimal information (faster performance)
Fully Exposed Type System
True = Server does expose the full Type System. False = Server doesn't provide the full type system. NOTE: A server is only required to expose any not well-defined types.
Alarms and Conditions
Supported Condition Types
AlarmConditionType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for AlarmConditionType and Derived Alarm Events
DiscrepancyAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for DiscrepancyAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
LimitAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for LimitAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
ExclusiveLimitAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for ExclusiveLimitAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
ExclusiveLevelAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for ExclusiveLevelAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
ExclusiveDeviationAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for ExclusiveDeviationAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
Exclusive Deviation Setpoint Source
Setpoint Nodes to monitor for ExclusiveDeviationAlarmType
ExclusiveRateOfChangeAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for ExclusiveRateOfChangeAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
NonExclusiveLimitAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for NonExclusiveLimitAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
NonExclusiveLevelAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for NonExclusiveLevelAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
NonExclusiveDeviationAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for NonExclusiveDeviationAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
NonExclusive Deviation Setpoint Source
Setpoint Nodes to monitor for NonExclusiveDeviationAlarmType
NonExclusiveRateOfChangeAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for NonExclusiveRateOfChangeAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
DiscreteAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for DiscreteAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
OffNormalAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for OffNormalAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
SystemOffNormalAlarmType Input Nodes
Input Nodes to monitor for SystemOffNormalAlarmType and Derived Alarm Events
Alarm Cycle Time
The time in seconds that an Alarm Condition will typically complete a cycle through all states
Time Tolerance
The time in milliseconds that an Event can be different from the Source data change
Maximum Event Count
The maximum number of events to keep before discarding
Chattering Alarms
Alarm ConditionIds that transition between active states only
Username of a user without access to the well-known role DiscoveryAdmin, the ApplicationAdmin Privilege or the ApplicationSelfAdmin Privilege
Password associated with NonAdministrativeUser
Username of a user with access to the well-known role <font color=blue>DiscoveryAdmin</font> or equivalent (<font color=blue>ApplicationAdmin</font>)
Password associated with AdministrativeUser
Username of a user with access to the ApplicationSelfAdmin Privilege, but without access to the well-known role DiscoveryAdmin and the ApplicationAdmin Privilege
Password associated with ApplicationSelfAdminUser
Username of a user with access to the SecurityAdmin role, but without access to the DiscoveryAdmin role, the ApplicationSelfAdmin Privilege or the ApplicationAdmin Privilege
Password associated with SecurityAdminUser
AliasName Discovery
AliasName Source 1 URL
Server URL of remote server #1, which should support and expose AliasNames
AliasName Source 1 UserName
UserName to use for login of remote server #1. This is only needed if an anonymous login is not possible.
AliasName Source 1 Password
Password to use for login of remote server #1. This is only needed if an anonymous login is not possible.
AliasName Source 2 URL
Server URL of remote server #2, which should support and expose AliasNames
AliasName Source 2 UserName
UserName to use for login of remote server #2. This is only needed if an anonymous login is not possible.
AliasName Source 2 Password
Password to use for login of remote server #2. This is only needed if an anonymous login is not possible.
Time to wait in milliseconds after each call of the RegisterApplication method for the AddressSpace to be refreshed (default=500)
The NetworkInterface [IP Address as string] to be used by the server under test for sending/receiving UDP PubSub messages.
The NetworkInterface [IP Address as string] to be used by the CTT for sending/receiving UDP PubSub messages.
The NetworkAddress Url of the device under test to be used in PubSubConfigurations
The NetworkAddress Url of the CTT to be used in PubSubConfigurations
The default size of String type DataSet fields to be used when generating connections with PeriodicFixed layout (needed to calculate DataSet Offsets)
The least SecurityMode that the server supports for PubSub communication (Method: Pull keys from CTT as SKS)
Chunking MaxNetworkMessageSize
The MaxNetworkMessageSize to be used for chunking tests. It must be set low enough for chunking to occur.

Blank [default]: If either Preconfiguration_CTT or this setting is left blank, the CTT will configure the PubSub server for each CU automatically using the PubSubConfiguration object or Add*-methods of the server
Configured: If both Preconfiguration_CTT and this setting are configured, the selected files will be used as PubSubConfiguration

Blank [default]: If either Preconfiguration_Server or this setting is left blank, the CTT will configure the PubSub server for each CU automatically using the PubSubConfiguration object or Add*-methods of the server
Configured: If both Preconfiguration_Server and this setting are configured, the selected files will be used as PubSubConfiguration
Limits the maximum time [sec] to wait for a sequence number roll-over to occur. If the estimated time is greater than this limit, according tests will be skipped. Default = 300 (5 min).

URL of the MQTT broker to be used for sending/receiving PubSub messages over MQTT.
If this setting is left empty, the CTT will use "opc.mqtt://[local hostname]:1883".
(default=empty)
The username to authenticate with, when connecting to the MQTT broker. If this setting is left empty, the CTT will connect as anonymous. (default=empty)
The password to be used along with the username above to authenticate on the MQTT broker.

Array of PublisherIds to ignore, in JSON format. PubSub messages with these PublisherIds will be discarded before processing. String Ids shall be noted in quotes.

Example: [123,"ExampleId",456,"123"]
Preferred PublisherId Type

Choose a preferred PublisherId type to use for generating connections if the default one is not supported
Default = UInt64

The Client Certificate to be used by the UA CTT when connecting to a UA Server.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
The Client's Private Key to be used by the UA CTT when connecting to a UA Server.

Trusted certificate that is valid, but has expired.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
Certificate private key that is valid, but has expired.

The Client Certificate (Algorithm=Sha1 | KeyLength=1024 Bit) to be used by the UA CTT when connecting to a UA Server.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
The Client's Private Key to be used by the UA CTT when connecting to a UA Server.

The Client Certificate (Algorithm=Sha1 | KeyLength=2048 Bit) to be used by the UA CTT when connecting to a UA Server.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
The Client's Private Key to be used by the UA CTT when connecting to a UA Server.

The Client Certificate (Algorithm=Sha256 | KeyLength=2048 Bit) to be used by the UA CTT when connecting to a UA Server.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
The Client's Private Key to be used by the UA CTT when connecting to a UA Server.

The Client Certificate (Algorithm=Sha256 | KeyLength=4096 Bit) to be used by the UA CTT when connecting to a UA Server.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
The Client's Private Key to be used by the UA CTT when connecting to a UA Server.

The Incorrectly Signed Certificate that the UA CTT should use when connecting to a UA Server.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
The Incorrectly signed Private key to use by the UA CTT when connecting to a UA Server.

Certificate that contains a hostname which does not match the computer.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
The private key of the certificate that contains an incorrect (non-matching) host name.

Certificate that does not contain the App URI of the CTT.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
The private key of the certificate that contains an incorrect App URI.

Certificate that is not yet valid.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
Private key for the certificate that is not yet valid.

Certificate that is NOT trusted by the Server.
Make sure your UA Server does not automatically trust Certificates!
Do not copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
Certificate (private key) that is NOT trusted by the Server. Make sure your UA Server does not automatically trust Certificates!

Expired certificate.
Do not copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
Expired Client Certificate, private key.

By the known CA issued certificate.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.

Do not copy this certificate to your Server's X509 UserIdentity trust list.

Public key for the trusted Certificate Authority.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trust list.
Private key for the trusted Certificate Authority.

Public key for a trusted CA where no revocation list is available.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's trusted list.

Public key for the untrusted but known Certificate Authority test cases.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's Issuers list.

Public key for a known CA where no revocationlist is available.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's issuers list.

Public key for a untrusted root CA.
Please do not copy this certificate to your Server's trusted list.

Public key for a trusted root CA but untrusted secondary CA.
Please do not copy this certificate to your Server's trusted list.

Public key for a trusted root CA where no revocation list is available and a known secondary CA.
Please copy this certificate to your Server's issuers list.

Location of the Certificate Trust List repository.
Recommended value: PKI/CA/certs
For advanced users only!
Location of the Revoked Certificate List repository.

Location of the issuer certificate list.
Recommended value: PKI/CA/issuers/certs

Valid syntax of an Node that is not valid.
This setting does not necessarily need to be changed. For advanced users only.

Valid syntax of an Node that is not valid.
This setting does not necessarily need to be changed. For advanced users only.

Valid syntax of an unknown Node.
This setting does not necessarily need to be changed. For advanced users only.

Valid syntax of an unknown Node.
This setting does not necessarily need to be changed. For advanced users only.

Valid syntax of an unknown Node.
This setting does not necessarily need to be changed. For advanced users only.

Valid syntax of an unknown Node.
This setting does not necessarily need to be changed. For advanced users only.

Valid syntax of an unknown Node.
This setting does not necessarily need to be changed. For advanced users only.

Invalid syntax of an Node.
This setting does not necessarily need to be changed. For advanced users only.

Invalid syntax of an Node.
This setting does not necessarily need to be changed. For advanced users only.

"http://opcfoundation.org/UA/", "urn:c-w-sum2:UA:Quickstarts:ReferenceServer", "http://opcfoundation.org/Quickstarts/ReferenceServer", "http://test.org/UA/Data/", "http://test.org/UA/Data/Instance", "http://opcfoundation.org/UA/Boiler/", "http://opcfoundation.org/UA/Boiler/Instance", "http://test.org/UA/Alarms/", "http://test.org/UA/Alarms/Instance", "http://opcfoundation.org/UA/Diagnostics", "http://samples.org/UA/MemoryBuffer", "http://samples.org/UA/MemoryBuffer/Instance"

The number of times to invoke an attribute services call:
Read, Write, HistoryRead, HistoryUpdate.
Default: 500

The number of times to invoke an attribute services call:
FindServers, GetEndpoints, RegisterServer.
Default: 5000

The number of times to invoke an attribute services call:
CreateMonitoredItems, ModifyMonitoredItems, SetMonitoringMode, SetTriggering, DeleteMonitoredItems.
Default: 500

The number of times to invoke an attribute services call:
AddNodes, AddReferences, DeleteNodes, DeleteReferences.
Default: 5000

The number of times to invoke an attribute services call:
CreateSession, ActivateSession, CloseSession, Cancel.
Default: 250

The number of times to invoke an attribute services call:
CreateSubscription, ModifySubscription, SetPublishingMode, Publish, Republish, DeleteSubscription, TransferSubscriptions.
Default: 500

The number of milleseconds (msecs) to wait in-between making Service calls.
Example: value=10; the Read test performs: Read, wait 10ms, Read, wait 10ms, Read etc.
Default value: 0. This means that there is no delay in-between Service calls.

The number of seconds to allow a Resource test-script to complete.
A repetitive script will stop when the hit-count (other settings above) is reached
or when the amount of time exceed exists this value, whichever occurs first.
Test Tool
Font Family
Courier New

The font used in the script editor.
Changes only affect newly opened editor windows.
Recommended value: Courier New
Font Size

The font size in points used in the script editor.
Changes only affect newly opened editor windows.
Recommended value: 8
Word Wrap

When checked, lines that are too long for the editor window are wrapped to a new line.
Changes only affect newly opened editor windows.
Recommended value: Clear

Checked=Log Warnings and Errors only.
Unchecked=Log ALL messages (warning: degrades performance).

Checked=Do not add timestmap to result entry.
This can be usefuly to diff results from several test runs.

Checked=Do not add any error details below testcase in result tree.
Unchecked=Also log error details.

Checked=Do not add any warning details below testcase in result tree.
Unchecked=Also log warning details.
Address Space Model
This value defines the maximum number of nodes in the offline address space cache of the server.\nNote: Increasing this value may lead to crashes of the CTT.
In case you do have a Mass folder exceeding the number of items defined above, you can exclude those nodes from the offline address space cache.
Max Browse Nodes

The MAXIMUM number nodes in one BrowseRequest. This setting is used when building an offline representation of the servers address space. This is only needed if not exposed in the server. Configure '0' if there is no maximum.

The MAXIMUM number of NodesToRead in one ReadRequest. This setting is used when building an offline representation of the servers address space. This is only needed if not exposed in the server. Configure '0' if there is no maximum.
The MAXIMUM number references to return for a BrowseRequest. This setting is used when building an offline representation of the servers address space. Configure '0' if there is no maximum.
Maximum number of retries to execute the BuildCacheMap service on error, before the operation is aborted (0 = No limit)
The maximum number of nodes that should be validated per test script. The setting is used e.g. in the Conformance Unit 'Base Info Type System'.
A delay (in seconds) that is used in some Conformance Units after each test script execution.

Checked=Do not show message boxes for not implemented or manual test cases.
Unchecked=Message boxes are shown and test run pauses.

Set this flag to reflect that the server is configured to suppress the verification of the certificate's validity regarding the expiry date/time. A server may be configured to allow connections using trusted but expired certificates.
When the CTT is run in the lab, none of these flags will be checked.

Set this flag to reflect that the server is configured to suppress errors caused by unavailable revocation list. If this flag is not set the server must have a revocation list (even an empty one) for each and every one trusted CA.
When the CTT is run in the lab, none of these flags will be checked.

Set this flag to reflect that the server is configured to ignore informational content of a certificate. Server can be configured to suppress set of optional uses of the certificate.
When the CTT is run in the lab, none of these flags will be checked.

Set this flag to reflect that the server is configured to ignore domain- and/or hostname mismatch when comparing the URL used to connect with the content of the certificate.
When the CTT is run in the lab, none of these flags will be checked.
Embedded Server Port
Port to be used by the embedded server of the CTT (default=4842)
Embedded Server Certificate
The Certificate to be used by the embedded server. Use this setting to initialize the embedded server instance with a certificate, other than the default one.
Embedded Server Private Key
The private key to be used by the embedded server. Use this setting to initialize the embedded server instance with a private key, other than the default one.
Embedded Server Trace Enabled
Enables trace logs for the embedded server (default=FALSE)
Endpoint Url

The endpointUrl of a Discovery Server.
TIP: Default LDS endpoint is: opc.tcp://localhost:4840

The level of Security to use when transmitting UA messages, such as None, Encrypt, Sign & Encrypt etc.
TIP: You should experiment with this setting if you are using a Custom UA Stack.
Recommended value: None

The default RequestedSecurityPolicyUri that the UA CTT will specify when connecting to a UA Server.
TIP: You should experiment with this setting if using a custom UA Stack.
Recommended value: None
Right-click and add your own settings and/or folders of settings.
Companion Specifications
OPC 30000 + 30001 - PLCOpen
NodeId of the object which contains the nodes to verify the mapping of datatypes.
OPC UA FX
VerificationMode Identity supported
True = Optional VerificationMode Identity is supported False = Optional VerificationMode Identity is not supported
VerificationMode IdentityAndCompatibility supported
True = Optional VerificationMode IdentityAndCompatibility is supported False = Optional VerificationMode IdentityAndCompatibility is not supported
AutomationML Container
AutomationML (AMLX) container file under test
